title: Crimes against Morality: Unintended Consequences of Criminalizing Sex Work
notes: Cameron, Seager, Shah (QJE 2021). Criminalizing sex work in Malang, East Java, Indonesia. Single treatment (Nov 2014), 2 periods (baseline Feb-Mar 2014, endline May-Jun 2015). Unit = worksite (wsid), 17 total but only 12 in sept_any sample. 6 criminalized (closing=1), 6 non-criminalized in STI sample. Outcome = sept_any (biological STI test positive, 0/1). Treatment variable dd_cl = closing * el (pre-constructed DiD composite). Stata: areg sept_any dd_cl el, abs(wsid) cluster(wsid). Table II Column 1 Panel A: beta=0.273, SE=0.101 (conventional clustered), N=459, baseline mean=0.465. Paper uses wild cluster bootstrap percentile-t p-values (Cameron, Gelbach, Miller 2008) because only 17 clusters. R feols with wsid+wave FEs gives identical coefficient 0.2726. Note: data_structure=repeated_cross_section because only 385 unique uid out of 459 obs (individuals enter/exit). With only 2 periods and single treatment timing, CS-NT reduces to standard DiD. SA disabled (single treatment timing, 2 periods). No event study (only 2 periods). IMPORTANT: conventional clustered SEs will differ from paper's reported bootstrap p-values. Paper reports *** significance via bootstrap, not conventional SEs.
